source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
title: Ensure Integrity Monitoring for Shielded GKE Nodes is Enabled

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
input_row.title: Ensure Integrity Monitoring for Shielded GKE Nodes is Enabled
input_row.description: Enable Integrity Monitoring for Shielded GKE Nodes to be notified of inconsistencies during the node boot sequence.
input_row.rationale: Integrity Monitoring provides active alerting for Shielded GKE nodes which allows administrators to respond to integrity failures and prevent compromised nodes from being deployed into the cluster. Impact: None.
input_row.audit: Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list 2. From the list of clusters, click on the name of the cluster under test. 3. Open the Details pane for each Node pool within the cluster, and ensure that 'Integrity monitoring' is set to 'Enabled' under the Security heading. Using Command Line: To check if Integrity Monitoring is enabled for the Node pools in the cluster, run the following command for each Node pool: gcloud container node-pools describe <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <compute_zone> --format json | jq .config.shieldedInstanceConfig This will return the following, if Integrity Monitoring is enabled: { "enableIntegrityMonitoring": true }
input_row.remediation: Once a Node pool is provisioned, it cannot be updated to enable Integrity Monitoring. New Node pools must be created within the cluster with Integrity Monitoring enabled. Using Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list 2. From the list of clusters, click on the cluster requiring the update and click ADD NODE POOL. 3. Ensure that the 'Integrity monitoring' checkbox is checked under the 'Shielded options' Heading. 4. Click SAVE. Workloads from existing non-conforming Node pools will need to be migrated to the newly created Node pool, then delete non-conforming Node pools to complete the remediation Using Command Line To create a Node pool within the cluster with Integrity Monitoring enabled, run the following command: gcloud container node-pools create <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <compute_zone> --shielded-integrity-monitoring Workloads from existing non-conforming Node pools will need to be migrated to the newly created Node pool, then delete non-conforming Node pools to complete the remediation Default Value: Integrity Monitoring is disabled by default on GKE clusters. Integrity Monitoring is enabled by default for Shielded GKE Nodes; however, if Secure Boot is enabled at creation time, Integrity Monitoring is disabled. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/shielded-gke-nodes 2. https://cloud.google.com/compute/shielded-vm/docs/integrity-monitoring

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
Based on the provided data and Google Cloud capabilities, the audit of this compliance control is best suited for an automated approach. The audit field clearly outlines using the Google Cloud CLI to verify the configuration of Integrity Monitoring on GKE nodes, which can be efficiently handled through GCP APIs/Google Cloud SDK without human judgment or SSH access.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_node_pool_integrity_monitoring_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command to describe the node pools within the GKE clusters:
  ```bash
  gcloud container node-pools describe <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <compute_zone> --format json
  ```
- Specifically, check the `shieldedInstanceConfig` field to verify that `enableIntegrityMonitoring` is set to `true`.
- Enumerate through all node pools within each GKE cluster for thorough coverage.
- Pass/Fail Criteria:
  - **Pass:** The `enableIntegrityMonitoring` is set to `true` for all node pools.
  - **Fail:** Any node pool detected with `enableIntegrityMonitoring` set to `false`.

The check involves listing all node pools and ensuring the configuration is present and correctly set, which directly aligns with the capabilities provided by the Google Cloud SDK and GCP APIs. This check verifies the Shielded GKE Nodes feature for better security at the infrastructure level, ensuring nodes are protected against integrity breaches.
